content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. It is worth 757575 points in division 111, 100100100 points in division 222, and is not scored in all other divisions.**

For an array BBB of length MMM, you are allowed to perform the following operation as many times as required:


Let S=‚àëi=1MBiS = \sum\limits_{i=1}^M{B_i}S=i=1‚àëM‚ÄãBi‚Äã
Pick iii such that 1‚â§i‚â§min‚Å°(M,‚à£S‚à£)1 \leq i \leq \min(M,|S|)1‚â§i‚â§min(M,‚à£S‚à£)
Either increment or decrement BiB_iBi‚Äã by 111
Define f(B)f(B)f(B) to be the minimum number of operations needed to turn every element of BBB to 000, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it's impossible to achieve.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN and QQQ queries.

For each query, given LiL_iLi‚Äã and RiR_iRi‚Äã, output the value of f(A[Li‚Ä¶Ri])f(A[L_i\ldots R_i])f(A[Li‚Äã‚Ä¶Ri‚Äã]), where A[Li‚Ä¶Ri]A[L_i\ldots R_i]A[Li‚Äã‚Ä¶Ri‚Äã] represents the subarray of AAA from the LiL_iLi‚Äã-th index to the RiR_iRi‚Äã-th index, that is, [ALi,ALi+1,‚Ä¶,ARi][A_{L_i}, A_{L_i + 1}, \ldots, A_{R_i}][ALi‚Äã‚Äã,ALi‚Äã+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ARi‚Äã‚Äã].


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ ‚Äî the number of elements of the array AAA and the number of queries, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers ‚Äî the elements of the array AAA.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. The iii-th of these QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi‚Äã and RiR_iRi‚Äã ‚Äî the subarray that needs to be considered for the iii-th query.




Output Format
For each query, output on a new line the value of f(A[Li‚Ä¶Ri])f(A[L_i\ldots R_i])f(A[Li‚Äã‚Ä¶Ri‚Äã]).
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot10^52‚ãÖ105.
